message:
fix(fsqlite-pager): publish post-rollback pager snapshot + regression test for shared-WAL extension visibility

pager.rs
  After certain commit paths we would end up with an inner pager state
  whose (commit_seq, db_size, journal_mode, freelist, checkpoint) was
  strictly ahead of what PublishedPager had most recently advertised.
  Readers that consulted the published snapshot to decide whether a
  peer's extension was visible could then see a stale visible_commit_seq
  or db_size and wrongly conclude the extension hadn't happened yet.
  Added two pieces:
    - Early in the commit prologue, bind bound_visible_commit_seq and
      bound_db_size to max(published_snapshot, inner) so the follow-up
      consistency-check path always has a value that honors "inner is
      at least as fresh as published."
    - After the commit body, re-snapshot PublishedPager and compute
      publication_lagged = any of (visible_commit_seq, db_size,
      journal_mode, freelist_count, checkpoint_active) is behind
      inner. If so, build PublishedPagerUpdate with per-field
      monotonic bumps and publish via publish_clear_if, passing
      clear_published_pages = true only when visible_commit_seq,
      db_size, or journal_mode actually changed — page cache entries
      need invalidation on those transitions but not on freelist /
      checkpoint-flag updates alone.

tests/self_alloc_extension_not_conflict.rs
  Added a SharedWalBackend harness (Arc<Mutex<Vec<(page, data, fsize)>>>)
  that two pagers can write into and read from, standing in for a
  real multi-writer WAL. The new regression test exercises the exact
  scenario where pager A extends the db via self-alloc, commits, and
  pager B — opened against the same shared WAL frames — then performs
  its own commit. Before this fix B's published snapshot could hold
  a stale visible_commit_seq and the `extension_not_conflict` invariant
  tripped; with the fix both sides observe monotonic
  (visible_commit_seq, db_size) and the test passes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
